title: Citation Verification Summary Entry
description: v3.11 #182 Delta 4 derived-view aggregate: one entry per (citation_key, ref_slug) pair, joining the v3.7.3 anchor signal, the v3.9.0/v3.11 triangulation unmatched signals, and the v3.8 audit verdicts into a single 'is this citation verified' view. The read interface for the §3.6 verification_gate contract (#183) and the #184 eval harness. Detection is unconditional (the aggregate is always populated so the signal is always visible, C-V6(e)); only the terminality is policy-gated by terminal_policies.citation_existence (Delta 3 / C-V6, a later batch). lookup_verified uses the v3.11 narrowed-false definition (C-V6(a)): false requires at least one ID-keyed unmatched, never a title-only unmatched. Spec docs/design/2026-05-21-v3.10-182-promote-citation-gate-spec.md §2 Delta 4 + §0(4a) + C-V6(a).

ref_slug.description: Joins to the <!--ref:slug--> markers used in writer prose.

lookup_verified.description: 3-class existence verdict (a STRING enum, never a JSON boolean). `true` iff at least one applicable resolver matched. `false` iff none matched and at least one exact identifier lookup (DOI, arXiv ID, or ADS bibcode) was unmatched. `unresolvable` covers outages, skipped resolvers, and title-only misses. The reducer in scripts/citation_verification_summary.py is the single source of truth.

anchor_present.description: True iff the citation's v3.7.3 anchor marker has kind in {quote, page, section, paragraph} (not none). Read by the #183 epistemic-status gate to know whether the row is anchorless.

verification_timestamp.description: ISO-8601 UTC. When >=1 resolver ran, the latest resolver timestamp; when all resolvers were skipped (manual entry) or all unreachable (outage), the attempt timestamp (when the summary was generated). Never null.

resolver_outcomes.description: Exactly five fields, one per resolver. A resolver that does not run carries status=skipped. queried_by records whether an exact identifier or title query was attempted for the narrowed-false reducer.

cache_age_days.description: #541 — citation-level age in days (rounded to 0.1) of the OLDEST live cache row for this citation across resolvers/query forms — deliberately conservative: an unexpired row for an obsolete query form can flag a citation whose latest verification is fresh (accepted advisory-direction false positive). Present only when at least one resolver outcome was served from cache. Never a gate input.

cache_stale_advisory.description: #541 — true when the emitted (rounded) cache_age_days exceeds ARS_CACHE_STALE_ADVISORY_DAYS (default 30; 0 disables). Advisory-only: surfaces as an ADV-CACHE row at the integrity checkpoint, never gates.

status.description: matched = resolver returned a match. unmatched = resolver ran and returned no match. unreachable = API outage / timeout. skipped = resolver did not run (manual entry per v3.7.3 exemption, or arXiv resolver on a non-arXiv citation per Delta 1).

queried_by.description: v3.11 #182 Delta 4 / C-V6(a): what the resolver actually queried by. REQUIRED (load-bearing for narrowed-false; an absent key would let an ambiguous unmatched silently reduce to unresolvable instead of false). `id` = an exact identifier (DOI or arXiv ID) lookup was attempted (so an `unmatched` here is ID-keyed = fabrication evidence). `title` = only a title search was possible (no resolvable identifier; an `unmatched` here is a coverage gap, reduces to unresolvable not false). `null` for skipped / pure-unreachable outcomes where no meaningful query completed. This reflects resolver EXECUTION, not entry shape (the entry having a DOI does not prove the DOI was queried — it may have been malformed/rejected).

response_summary.description: Optional human-readable summary of the resolver response (e.g. matched title, error class). Null when not captured.

allOf.description: A ran resolver (matched / unmatched) always knows what it keyed on: queried_by MUST be id or title, never null. This closes the exact ambiguity the narrowed-false reducer can be fooled by (a null-keyed unmatched).
A resolver that did not complete a query (skipped / unreachable) MUST carry queried_by=null — there was no meaningful query to attribute.
